description: This figure presents electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) inverse pole figure (IPF) orientation maps and matching {100}, {110}, {111} pole figures for Fe-28Mn-8Al-1C low-density austenitic steel under three cryogenic rolling (CR) deformation states. Subfigure (a) corresponds to the undeformed sample (scale bar 50 μm), showing equiaxed austenite grains with random crystallographic orientation. Subfigure (b) corresponds to the sample with 30% CR thickness reduction (scale bar 20 μm), exhibiting partially elongated grains and the formation of copper {112}<111> texture. Subfigure (c) corresponds to the sample with 90% CR reduction (scale bar 5 μm), showing heavily fragmented, fibrous grains elongated along the rolling direction, with texture transformed to brass {110}<112> type. The results demonstrate that increasing CR deformation progressively refines grains, elongates microstructure along the rolling direction, and drives systematic texture evolution, which links to the improved strength and reduced ductility of deformed samples, supporting that CR effectively modulates the microstructure and texture of the studied low-density steel.
